name: language-progress passes subjectId path param to service with no UUID validation
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L8-09] getCurrentLanguageProgress(db, profileId, c.req.param('subjectId')) — route has no zValidator, unlike sibling routes. Fix: zValidator('param', {subjectId: uuid}).
acceptanceCriteria:
Root cause: GET /subjects/:subjectId/cefr-progress (apps/api/src/routes/language-progress.ts:26) passes c.req.param('subjectId') directly to getCurrentLanguageProgress with no zValidator middleware, unlike sibling routes that validate path params with zValidator('param', ...).

Variants: single variant — the :subjectId path parameter is the only unvalidated ID in this route; a non-UUID string (e.g. 'abc') reaches the service unchecked.

Fix: add zValidator('param', z.object({ subjectId: z.string().uuid() })) before the async handler and read via c.req.valid('param').subjectId.

Regression test: in apps/api/src/routes/language-progress.integration.test.ts (or a new unit test in language-progress.test.ts), add a test that calls GET /v1/subjects/not-a-uuid/cefr-progress and expects HTTP 400. Without the fix the route passes the invalid ID to the service; with the fix it returns 400 before calling getCurrentLanguageProgress. Run: cd apps/api && pnpm exec jest --testPathPattern=routes/language-progress --no-coverage.
riskImpact: Non-UUID flows into a DB query; diverges from every sibling route.
